channel.title: Lee's Blog
channel.description: Thoughts on literature, design, and the quiet spaces in between.

item.title: The Weight of Silence in Modern Fiction
item.description: In an age of constant noise, the most powerful literary moments are often those that say nothing at all.

item.title: Brutalism Revisited: Form Follows Friction
item.description: Why the raw, unpolished aesthetic of brutalist design continues to resonate in a world obsessed with perfection.

item.title: Solitude as a Creative Practice
item.description: The relationship between being alone and making meaningful work.

item.title: The Craft of Slow Typing
item.description: On the lost art of composing at the speed of thought.

item.title: On the Surface of Water
item.description: Stillness is not the absence of movement but the presence of a deeper current beneath.

item.title: The Patina of Old Books
item.description: What worn spines and yellowed pages can teach us about time, memory, and the materiality of reading.
